summary: A solid audience tape delivers a scorching Dark Star that melts into an epic Morning Dew, with only a few slip‑ups in Sugar Magnolia.
review: The recording is an audience capture (Bear’s famous AUD) that sounds deep and clear despite some hiss and occasional cuts. Musically the show shines most on the massive Dark Star → Morning Dew sequence, which reviewers describe as intense, explosive and near‑perfect, while Sugar Magnolia suffers from noticeable sloppiness.

ai_rating.rationale: Five detailed reviews overwhelmingly praise the performance (especially Dark Star/Morning Dew) and rate the audio well; minor criticisms do not outweigh the strong positives.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Deep, clear sound with some hiss and occasional cuts (e.g., Casey Jones ending); overall a top‑tier 1972 AUD tape.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Second set builds power, highlighted by the 45‑minute Dark Star → Morning Dew stretch; despite missing portions of the show the flow remains strong and engaging.

band_member_comments.Phil: Delivers stomping bass lines and an outstanding solo during the climax of Dark Star.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith leads out of the dark jam, providing a less ominous feel before Phil takes over.
